attractions.name: Schwarzmönch
attractions.description: A massive, dramatic cliff face known as the 'Black Monk' providing a stunning backdrop to the valley.

attractions.name: Schilthorn Summit
attractions.description: High-altitude summit offering panoramic views of the Bernese Alps, accessible via cable car from Gimmelwald.

attractions.name: Birg Cable Car Station
attractions.description: Intermediate station on the way to Schilthorn, known for its cliff walk and dramatic mountain views.

attractions.name: Allmendhubel
attractions.description: A scenic mountain ridge above Mürren, accessible by funicular, offering flower trails and playgrounds.

attractions.name: Winteregg
attractions.description: A beautiful plateau area between Grütschalp and Mürren with excellent views of the Eiger, Mönch, and Jungfrau.

attractions.name: Staubbach Falls
attractions.description: Iconic waterfall in Lauterbrunnen that plunges nearly 300 meters from a hanging valley.

attractions.name: Trümmelbach Falls
attractions.description: A series of ten glacier-fed waterfalls inside the mountain, accessible via a tunnel lift.

attractions.name: Jungfraujoch
attractions.description: The 'Top of Europe' railway station, offering a high-alpine experience with ice palaces and glacier views.

restaurants.name: Mountain Hostel Restaurant
restaurants.description: Rustic dining spot in Gimmelwald, open to non-residents, serving hearty local fare.

restaurants.name: Pension Gimmelwald Restaurant
restaurants.description: Traditional chalet-style restaurant offering authentic Swiss cuisine with valley views.

restaurants.name: Gimmeln Restaurant
restaurants.description: Mountain restaurant located on the hiking trail between Gimmelwald and Schilthorn.

restaurants.name: Schiltalp Restaurant
restaurants.description: A remote alpine hut serving refreshments to hikers on the way to the Schilthorn ridge.

hidden_gems.name: Tanzbödeli
hidden_gems.description: A glacial cirque and plateau offering a unique, quiet vantage point over the Lauterbrunnen valley.

hidden_gems.name: Oberhornsee
hidden_gems.description: A picturesque, secluded alpine lake nestled in meadows, accessible via a challenging hike.

nightlife.name: Mountain Hostel Bar
nightlife.description: The primary social hub in Gimmelwald, offering a relaxed atmosphere for hikers and travelers.

shopping.name: Mürren Village Shops
shopping.description: The nearest hub for groceries, sports equipment, and souvenirs, located 3km uphill.

adventure_activities.name: Via Ferrata Mürren-Gimmelwald
adventure_activities.description: A thrilling mountain climbing route that descends from Mürren to Gimmelwald with spectacular exposure.

local_tips: Buy fresh Alpkäse (alpine cheese) and yoghurt directly from local farmers in Gimmelwald.
Drink freely from the public water troughs; the mountain water is fresh and safe.
Gimmelwald is car-free; use the Stechelberg cable car to arrive.
Check the weather frequently; conditions can change rapidly on the Schilthorn ascent.

safety.general: Mountain weather is unpredictable; always carry appropriate gear even in summer.
safety.water_safety: Public troughs provide safe, fresh drinking water.
safety.transport_safety: Ensure you catch the last cable car or bus connection, as late-night options are limited.

culture.overview: Gimmelwald is a traditional, quiet farming hamlet with a strong emphasis on preserving Alpine heritage.
culture.etiquette: Respect private farm land and keep to marked hiking trails. Always close gates behind you if passing through pastures.

transport.getting_around: The village is small enough to walk everywhere. Access to Mürren is via a 30-minute walk or the cable car.

weather.best_time_to_visit: Summer (June to September) for hiking, winter for skiing.
